references.title: GIKT: A Graph-based Interaction Model for Knowledge Tracing

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Probability of getting the next candidate exercise correct
extracted.Students: 3852 ASSISTments09 learners, 27485 ASSISTments12 learners nad 5000 EdNet learners
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Previous models often compute the learners’ current state as a weighted sum aggregation of all historical states based on correlation between current question and historical questions → all previous knowledge states are considered.
extracted.Novelty of Model: The model GIKT uses attention to select which previous states should be considered for the computation of new question performance. Moreover, it is, to the authors’ knowledge to evaluate question-skills relationships via Graph Neural Networks.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The model creates 3 embedding matrices that represent questions, skills and answers.

Question-skill relationships are modelled using a Knowledge Graph. 

The model incorporates the concept of mastery of a question by the student. This mastery is determined from the history of interactions with similar questions.
extracted.Models: BKT, KTM, DKT, DKVMN, DKT-Q, DKT-QS, GAKT
extracted.Comments: Authors propose a new model and validate it using 3 datasets. The authors perform a set of Ablation studies to show how parameter optimization impacts the performance of the models.
extracted.Limitations:
No testing on Cold-Start Scenario.

No Cross-Validation.

annotations.text: KT in online settings has some differences to other contexts, as online questions are often associated with a smaller set of skills but tend to be more and more common. Thus, KT works in online settings could explore problems that sparse dataset cannot - such as question-skill correlations.

To solve this limitation, authors propose a Graph-based Interaction model for KT. The model uses Graph Convolutional NNs to include question-skill correlations. Essencially, the model can identify the key questions for skill mastery and consider how it evolves from the moment the key questions were done to new questions related with this skill. Results show that GIKT achieves new State-of-the-art performance.
